title: Spec review session: commit and push completed changes
description:
This session completed several changes that need committing:

Done:
1. ActionFlowStep type fix (src/spec/types.ts, src/agent/executor.ts, src/e2e/spec-to-test.ts, src/validation/validator.ts)
2. Consolidated spec refine into spec evolve (src/cli/commands/spec-evolve.ts, spec-refine.ts, commands-manifest.ts, index.ts)
3. Added standalone capture command (src/cli/commands/capture.ts, index.ts, commands-manifest.ts)
4. Added 7 error-path tests to self-spec (specify.spec.yaml)
5. Created narrative format (src/spec/narrative.ts) - NEW FILE
6. Created interviewer engine (src/spec/interviewer.ts) - NEW FILE
7. Created create command (src/cli/commands/create.ts) - NEW FILE

All changes type-check clean. Self-spec passes 118/118 assertions.

Need to: git add, commit, push.

title: Wire up specify create command + narrative format

The interviewer engine (src/spec/interviewer.ts), narrative format (src/spec/narrative.ts), and create command (src/cli/commands/create.ts) are already written but not wired into the CLI.

Remaining work:
1. Add routing in src/cli/index.ts for 'create' command
2. Add command definition in src/cli/commands-manifest.ts
3. Add 'narrative_path' optional field to Spec type in src/spec/types.ts
4. Add narrative_path to spec JSON schema in src/spec/schema.ts
5. Add self-spec tests for the create command
6. Update help text to include create
7. Test the full interview flow end-to-end

title: Product identity: deterministic CLI vs LLM-native hybrid
description:
Specify needs an identity decision. All spec generation/evolution/refinement is currently heuristic-only (no LLM calls). The tool is most powerful in symbiosis with an LLM.

Options:
A) Stay deterministic, LLMs call Specify via MCP/CLI (current approach)
B) Add optional --llm flag for commands that benefit from intelligence (recommended)
C) Go fully LLM-native with Anthropic SDK

Recommendation: Hybrid (B). Keep core deterministic, add optional LLM layer:
  specify create                        # structured interview (no LLM)
  specify create --llm                  # conversational interview (uses LLM)
  specify spec evolve --pr 42 --llm     # deep semantic PR analysis
  specify spec generate --smart --llm   # vision-aided spec from screenshots

All current commands (validate, lint, export, import, sync, cli run, agent run, capture, report diff/stats, schema, guide) are fully deterministic. generate/evolve use heuristics but no LLM.

title: Narrative ↔ spec sync: keep human and computable specs aligned
description:
Design and implement a mechanism to keep the narrative companion (.narrative.md) in sync with the computable spec (.yaml).

Ideas:
- spec lint could warn when narrative refs point to nonexistent spec items
- spec evolve could suggest narrative updates alongside spec changes
- specify site could highlight stale sections where spec changed but narrative didn't
- A specify narrative sync command that diffs the two and reports mismatches
- Bidirectional: if someone edits the narrative (adds a new section with a spec:page:foo ref), the tool could scaffold the corresponding spec items

The narrative format already supports spec refs via <!-- spec:page:login --> comments.
src/spec/narrative.ts already has serialize/parse.

title: Static site generator: specify site --spec <path>
description:
Generate a self-contained static HTML site from a spec + optional narrative companion.

Features:
- index.html: overview with spec summary, pages, flows, CLI commands
- Per-page detail pages with assertions, scenarios, API contracts
- Per-flow detail pages with step visualization
- Narrative rendering alongside computable spec items (linked via spec:refs)
- Validate buttons that POST to a running specify MCP server or invoke CLI
- Test result display inline (pass/fail/untested badges)

Command: specify site --spec <path> --output <dir> [--narrative <path>] [--url <target>]

New files needed:
- src/cli/commands/site.ts
- src/site/generator.ts (main site generation)
- src/site/templates.ts (HTML templates as template literals)
- src/site/styles.ts (embedded CSS)

Progressive enhancement: ship static reference first, add interactive validation later.

Depends on: narrative format (src/spec/narrative.ts - already created)
